data.content: I can see your project — the express delivery test expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m`. Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically on file changes:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload on their test project. Let me start it up for them using async mode so they can iterate.

Looking at the tests, I can see the express test will fail because `CalculateShipping("express", 1.0)` returns `10.00m` but the test expects `15.00m`.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode.

data.content: There's a missing `using System;` preventing compilation. Let me fix that and then start the watch:
data.reasoningText:
There's a build error - `ArgumentException` needs `using System;`. Let me fix that first, then set up the watch. Actually, the user said they want me to set up hot reload - they didn't ask me to fix the tests. Let me fix the build error and then set up the watch.

Actually, let me re-read the request. The user says "I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?" So they want the hot reload set up. Let me fix the compilation error first (since it won't work otherwise) and then start the watch.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ **Passed:** `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5`
- ❌ **Failed:** `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expects `15.00` but gets `10.00`

The watcher is active in shell `watch-tests`. Any time you save a change to `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, the tests will automatically re-run. For example, to fix the failing test, change `"express" => 10.00m` to `"express" => 15.00m` in the `CalculateShipping` method.
